DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jonathan Mason Plaintiff (317.508.4984; lwyrupblacke@gmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Annie Lau, Esq. 415.490.9000; alau@fisherphillips.com) of Fisher & Phillips LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JONATHAN MASON'S DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's demurrer to defendants' answer is sustained with leave to amend.

It is common for answers to laundry-list affirmative defenses - common but not correct. Answers must aver facts "as carefully and with as much detail as the facts which constitute the cause of action and are alleged in the complaint." (FPI Development, Inc. v. Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384; Edmon and Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) 6:459; Opp. 8:18-20.) Here, the answer avers 33 affirmative defenses that are legal labels and little more.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2022 Continued To Apr-26-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
